summary: Hypothesis report outlining a rational drug design strategy to develop stabilized analogs of kisspeptin-10 (KP-10) peptide with improved metabolic stability and pharmacokinetics while maintaining KISS1R receptor agonism. The document describes known liabilities of native KP-10, proposes three analog classes with specific modification strategies, and outlines an experimental plan for validation. This is a pre-experimental planning document establishing scientific rationale for TRL 1-2 work.

content_blocks.title: Core Hypothesis
content_blocks.content: Rationally modified derivatives of kisspeptin-10 (KP-10), based on native sequence YNWSFGLRF-NH2, may retain KISS1R receptor agonism while demonstrating improved metabolic stability and longer systemic exposure. Native KP-10 validates receptor compatibility, with optimization shifting toward protease resistance and pharmacokinetics.

content_blocks.title: Known Liabilities of Native KP-10
content_blocks.content: Identified vulnerabilities: aminopeptidase attack at Y1 position, cleavage near F6/G7, trypsin sensitivity near R9/F10, and rapid clearance. These are the specific degradation mechanisms to be addressed through analog design.

content_blocks.title: Analog Class A: N-terminal Protection
content_blocks.content: Strategy for N-terminal protection via three approaches: acetylation, D-Tyr1 substitution, or pyroglutamic acid (pyroGlu) capping. Targets aminopeptidase attack at Y1.

content_blocks.title: Analog Class B: Cleavage-Site Hardening
content_blocks.content: Strategy to prevent internal cleavage via N-methyl substitutions, D-amino acid swaps, or beta-amino acid insertion. Targets protease sites at F6/G7 and R9/F10.

content_blocks.title: Analog Class C: Conformational Locking
content_blocks.content: Strategy for structural stabilization via cyclization, stapling, or lactam bridges to lock peptide conformation and potentially improve stability.

content_blocks.title: Experimental Plan
content_blocks.content: Proposed workflow: design analogs, pipeline screen, molecular docking studies, serum stability assays, and signaling assays. T1-01 calibration mentioned as enabling direct benchmarking of future candidates.

content_blocks.title: Commercial Applications
content_blocks.content: Target indications identified: fertility, endocrinology, oncology signaling, and metabolic regulation. Positioning: Native KP-10 as proof-of-biology, engineered analogs as potential proof-of-therapy.

content_blocks.title: Comparative Metrics: Native KP-10 vs Hypothesis Analog
content_blocks.content: Plasma half-life: Native KP-10 = Low, Hypothesis Analog = Higher. Protease resistance: Native = Low, Analog = Moderate/High. KISS1R potency: Native = High, Analog = Maintained/Slightly reduced. Dosing frequency: Native = High, Analog = Lower. Manufacturing complexity: Native = Low, Analog = Higher. This table establishes target product profile showing trade-offs between improved stability/PK versus increased manufacturing complexity, with acceptable potency maintenance.

key_findings: This is a hypothesis/planning document with no experimental data yet generated - establishes scientific rationale for TRL 1
Native KP-10 sequence (YNWSFGLRF-NH2) serves as starting point with validated KISS1R agonism
Three distinct analog modification strategies proposed: N-terminal protection (Class A), cleavage-site hardening (Class B), and conformational locking (Class C)
Specific degradation mechanisms identified: aminopeptidase at Y1, cleavage at F6/G7, trypsin sensitivity at R9/F10
Experimental validation plan outlined: docking, serum stability assays, signaling assays with T1-01 as benchmark
Target product profile prioritizes improved plasma half-life and protease resistance while maintaining KISS1R potency
Commercial applications span fertility, endocrinology, oncology, and metabolic regulation
No actual experimental results, efficacy data, or manufactured compounds reported - purely conceptual stage
